q: The environmental problems directly related to energy production and consumption include air pollution, climate change, water pollution, solid waste disposal, and ___ pollution.
Global warming refers to the gradual increase in the average temperature of the earth's surface and its atmosphere due to the accumulation of ___ gases.
Which of the following greenhouse gases also destroys the earth's protective ozone layer?
Acid rains are caused mainly by the release of sulphur dioxide and ___ when fossil fuels burn.
The ability of carbon dioxide alone to generate true acid rain is very limited because carbonic acid is a relatively ___ acid.
The oil industry adds ___ to petrol (gasoline) to help engines run more smoothly.
A tidal barrage is placed at the entrance to a bay or ___.
Biomass pyrolysis produces fuels such as charcoal, bio-oil, renewable diesel, hydrogen, and ___.
A PSH facility stores energy by pumping water to an upper reservoir when the demand for electricity is ___.
In a wind turbine, the propeller-like blades catch wind energy and change kinetic energy into ___ energy.
The problems posed by acid rains include corrosion of the built environment, water pollution, depletion of forests, and soil ___.
The problems posed by acid rains include corrosion of the built environment, soil degradation, water pollution and depletion of ___.
The oil industry adds ___ to petrol (gasoline) to help engines run more smoothly.
Smoke from the combustion of leaded fuels in vehicles causes cancer and high blood pressure in ___.
In children, combustion of leaded fuels impairs mental development, reduces intelligence and causes ___.
Excavated areas that have been strip mined for coal but are not filled and revegetated cause ___ pollution as surface water runoff.
Surface water runoff from the mined area can flush sediments and ___ compounds into nearby streams and rivers.
Emissions from vehicles, thermal power plants and factories contain unburned hydrocarbons, particulates, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and oxides of nitrogen and ___.
Over its estimated life a ___ module will produce much more electricity than was used in its production.
Photovoltaic systems make no noise and cause no ___ while in operation.
A wind farm occupies less land area per ___ of electricity generated than any other energy conversion system.
Low frequency sound and ___ are usually beneath the threshold of human hearing.
Modern turbine designs which locate the blades ___ instead of downwind have significantly reduced the level of infrasound.
Wind turbines may create ___ on residences when the sun passes behind the turbine.
Although hydropower has no ___ impacts, construction and operation of hydropower dams can significantly affect natural river systems.
In flat basins large dams cause ___ of large tracts of land, destroying local animals and habitats.
Large amounts of plant life submerged by dams decay ___.
Large dams are breeding grounds for mosquitoes and cause the spread of ___.
Dams serve as a heat sink, water in dams is ___ than the normal river water.
Geothermal power is a relatively ___ source of energy.
Electricity generation from ___ resources involves much lower greenhouse gas emission rates than that from fossil fuels.
Biomass power plants share some similarities with fossil fuel power plants: both involve the combustion of a ___ to generate electricity.
Biomass power plants, like coal and natural gas-fired power plants, require water for ___.
For solar energy, the energy transfer is from light & thermal energy $\rightarrow$ ___ energy.
For hydroelectricity, the energy transfer is from ___ energy $\rightarrow$ kinetic energy $\rightarrow$ electrical energy.
A ___ resource is a natural substance that is not replenished with the speed at which it is consumed.
Renewable energy sources are also called ___ energy sources.
___ energy is the radiant light and heat from the sun that has been harnessed by humans since ancient times.
Geothermal energy is a type of renewable energy taken from the Earth's ___.
Acid rain is caused by a chemical reaction that begins when compounds like ___ and nitrogen oxides are released into the air.
These substances can rise very high into the atmosphere, where they mix and react with water, oxygen, and other chemicals to form more acidic ___.
